Title: PSG beat Arsenal on penalties to retain Champions League title
Subhead: Paris Saint-Germain and Arsenal went to a penalty shoot-out in Budapest on Saturday after a 1-1 draw, then PSG beat the Gunners with a final score of 4-3.

Title: Putin's war fuels the growing role of Russia's FSB intelligence agency
Mexico's secret cooperation with the US on deportations exposed in new report
Palestinian refugees forced to flee the southern Lebanon: 'All my life, I have only known displacement'
Subhead: Evacuation orders from the Israeli army, which signal incoming bombardments, now include for the first time all three Palestinian camps in the Tyre region. Issued on Wednesday and renewed the following day, these orders forced thousands of Palestinian refugees to flee their homes.

Title: SoftBank to spend $87.5 billion on AI centers in France
Subhead: The company's founder Masayoshi Son, 68, said he made the decision after meeting French President Emmanuel Macron during a visit to Tokyo in April and that France's status as an energy exporter had played a key role. Data centers are huge consumers of energy.

Title: Paul McCartney blends rock and melancholic ballads in 'The Boys of Dungeon Lane,' his 20th solo album
Subhead: The Beatles' co-founder invited the band's drummer, Ringo Starr, for a duet on the album. Starr, meanwhile, released his own record in April.

Title: José Tolentino de Mendonça, the poet cardinal reconciling the Church and contemporary art
Subhead: The Portuguese prelate has become a prominent figure in the world of contemporary art, befriending artists and film stars. As the driving force behind the Vatican Pavilion at the recently opened Venice Biennale, he has used his progressive outlook to help bridge the Church and a world that long viewed it with suspicion.

Who will manage to capture the 'middle' electorate Macron seized 10 years ago?
Subhead: Among French President Emmanuel Macron's political heirs, and even on the left, some are starting to believe that the presidential campaign could play out in the middle, amid the collapse of France's former governing parties.

Title: The Bolloré protégée spreading Russian propaganda in France
Subhead: Xenia Fedorova, the former director of the now-banned Russian channel RT France, has free rein to advance the Kremlin's version of events without contradiction across the media empire of far-right billionaire Vincent Bolloré.

Title: Iran is gradually reconnecting to the internet, though access remains limited
Subhead: After a three-month blackout, Iran regained partial internet access on Tuesday, May 26. The Intelligence Ministry has warned Iranians against collaborating with any foreign media, which it accused of espionage.
